mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future equity cash flows, mechanically depressing S&P 500 valuations and generating negative forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of months where FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0%, labor market slack reduces corporate wage pressures and signals weakening consumer demand, causing S&P 500 forward returns to turn negative over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of observations where UNRATE > 4.0%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real sales of automobiles and rising consumer prices indicates strong demand outpacing supply capacity, which boosts corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and CPIAUCSL both rise, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production signals immediate supply-side momentum that translates into positive short-term S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days, but only during periods of stable monetary policy.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction or becomes insignificant during stable monetary policy periods, or 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rising INDPRO months.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which directly supports corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year window, or positive payroll growth correlates with negative 20-day returns in >60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 1300 thousand units, it signals a sharp contraction in construction activity and related consumer spending, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in >65% of months where HOUST < 1300, or the threshold effect disappears in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation rises faster than the federal funds rate, real interest rates turn negative, stimulating risk appetite and equity valuations, resulting in positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The spread between CPIAUCSL growth and FEDFUNDS fails to predict returns, or positive real rate differentials correlate with negative equity returns in >58% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.0% confirms a robust economic expansion that sustains corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days, but only when unemployment remains below 4.5%.
failure_condition: The GDP signal loses predictive power or flips negative when UNRATE < 4.5%, or 60-day forward returns are negative in >60% of high-GDP quarters under this regime.
